title: Life and Works in Rizal (Rizal 102) Midterm Examination

questions.question: Rizal's nationalistic essay and his first article that is written on Spain's soil.

options.d: A La Senorita C. O. y P.

options.a: to observe keenly the life and culture of the European people.
options.b: to observe their languages and customs
options.c: to observe their government and laws of the European nations.

questions.question: A French steamer which left Singapore for Europe on May 11. It was a larger and cleaner vessel which carried more passengers.
What was Rizal's first impression of Barcelona.

options.b: He found it as a great city

questions.question: This Italian City pleased him because of its business activity, its lively people, and its panoramic beauty.
The following are Rizal's articles, except one.
While sojourning in Barcelona, Rizal received sad news about

options.a: measles in Manila and unhappiness of Ms. L
options.b: cholera ravaging and unhappiness of Leonor Rivera
options.c: cholera ravaging and unhappiness of Segunda Katigbak
options.d: measles in Madrid and happiness of Ms. L.

questions.question: What University Rizal enrolled with in which he took up two courses-Medicine and Philosophy & Letters.
Don Pablo's daughter who fell in love with Rizal.
A sad poem of Rizal which he personally declaimed during the New Year's Eve reception of the Madrid Filipinos.

While living in Paris, Rizal worked as an assistant to leading French ophthalmologist.

A famous German anthropologist who recognized Rizal's genius.
A German scientist-traveler and author of Travels in the Philippines.
Friend of Rizal the savior of Noli who was a scion of a rich family of San Miguel, Bulacan. He helped Rizal finance the printing cost of Noli.
When was Noli Me Tangere finally finished and ready for printing.
The name of the printing shop which charged the lowest rate for the reproduction of the novel.
The ________ is a Latin phrase which means 'Touch Me Not'.
How many chapters does the novel Noli Me Tangere have?
The place where Rizal had beautiful memories of his visit, he enjoyed the warm hospitality of the Blumentritt family.
A city where Rizal fascinated with because of its beautiful buildings, religious images, haunting waltzes and majestic charm.
The 'Eternal City' and also called the 'City of the Caesars'
The following reasons why Rizal determined to return to the Philippines, except one.

options.a: to operate on his mother's eye
options.b: to serve his people who had long been oppressed by Spanish tyrants
options.c: to inquire why Leonor Rivera remained silent.
options.d: to help people improve their livelihood

questions.question: The Governor General who wrote a letter requesting Rizal to come to Malacanan Palace to explain his novel subversive ideas.
A Filipino Catholic priest-scholar, a theologian and Tagalog translator who have a brilliant defense of the novel Noli Me Tangere.
The age of Rizal when he became an embittered victim of human iniquities, a disillusioned dreamer and frustrated reformer.
Three reasons why Rizal did not get off his ship at Amoy on February 7, 1888, except one.

options.c: he heard that the city has many Spaniards
options.d: he heard that the city was dirty

questions.question: On ________ Rizal left Hongkong on board the Oceanic, an American steamer.
A city where Rizal described in his diary as small, low and gloomy. It looks sad and is almost dead.
A Spaniard and a former secretary of Governor General Terrero who shadowed Rizal's movement in Hongkong.
One of the happiest interludes in the life of Rizal.

options.c: His sojourn in the Land of Cherry Blossoms for 1½ month
options.d: His sojourn in the Land of Tokyo Japan

questions.question: The real name of a Japanese girl whom Rizal fell in love with.
Why did Rizal felt embarrassed during his first day in Tokyo Japan?

options.a: because he has no money for shopping
options.b: because the diplomatic authorities spy his movements.
options.c: because he did not know the Japanese language.
options.d: because he sacrificed his happiness for his girl.

questions.question: One thing which Rizal did not like in Japan.

options.a: The beggars seen in the city streets of Japan.
options.c: The popular mode of transportation by means of rickshaws by men.
options.d: The picturesque dress of Japanese women.

questions.question: Things which favorably impressed Rizal in Japan.

options.b: The cleanliness and politeness of the Japanese people.

questions.question: A fighting Japanese, journalist and champion of human rights whom Rizal befriended with on board the Belgic.
